remember: Students will be able to recall the Thomson model of an atom, Rutherford's nuclear model of an atom and the Bohr's atomic model.
Students will be able to list the basic features of Thomson's model of an atom, Rutherford's nuclear model and Bohr's atomic model.
Students will be able to state the limitations of Thomson's model of an atom.
Students will be able to explain the significance of Rutherford's nuclear model of an atom.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of electron volt (eV) and its relation to the energy of an electron.
Students will be able to explain the concept of ionization energy, electron affinity, and electronegativity.
Students will be able to describe the energy quantization in terms of the ground state and excited states of an atom.
Students will be able to explain the concept of electron spin and its significance.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the velocity of an electron in the nth orbit of a hydrogen atom.
Students will be able to calculate the energy of an electron in the nth orbit of a hydrogen atom.
Students will be able to calculate the radius of the nth orbit of a hydrogen atom.
analyze: Students will be able to compare and contrast the different models of an atom.
Students will be able to explain the importance of the atomic models in understanding the structure of atoms.

ncert_objectives: Explain the Rutherford's nuclear model of an atom and its significance.
Describe the Bohr's atomic model of an atom and explain its limitations.
Explain the concept of electron volt (eV) and its relation to the energy of an electron.
Explain the energy quantization in terms of the ground state and excited states of an atom.
board_exam_objectives: Recall the basic features of Thomson's model of an atom, Rutherford's nuclear model, and Bohr's atomic model.
Identify the limitations of Thomson's model of an atom.
Calculate the velocity of an electron in the nth orbit of a hydrogen atom.
Explain the concept of electron spin and its significance.
